name: Hermeto Pascoal
description: Brazilian composer, musician and record producer (1936–2025)
abstract: Hermeto Pascoal (22 June 1936 – 13 September 2025) was a Brazilian composer and multi-instrumentalist. Pascoal was best known in Brazilian music for his orchestration and improvisation, as well as for being a record producer and contributor to many Brazilian and international albums.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: He flew me out here from New York. I remember playing that three times... His name was in the mix. So they made sure that I got the call.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: he would bring records by Hermeto Pascoal and when I heard Milton Nascimento and people like that, in the early seventies.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Anat Cohen
about_quotes.evidence: my friend Jovino Santos Neto he is a great musician, he played for years with Hermeto Pascoal
about_quotes.doc_title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Bunnett
about_quotes.evidence: And then we got to work with the great Hermeto Pascoal. Had a big concert here with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Bunnett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 25, 2024
